scale.meaning: 0 means no supporting evidence; 5 means strong, current, cross-checked evidence. Scores are research aids, not investment ratings.

demand_pressure.description: Strength, persistence, and visibility of demand pressure.

throughput_impact.description: Degree to which the node limits defined system throughput.

ccr_characteristics.description: Evidence of capacity-constrained-resource behavior such as queues, allocation, or schedule control.

substitution_difficulty.description: Technical, qualification, switching-cost, ecosystem, or regulatory barriers to substitution.

elevation_lead_time.description: Time and uncertainty required to expand, debottleneck, qualify, or otherwise elevate supply.

subordination_evidence.description: Evidence that upstream and downstream actors adapt plans, inventory, or schedules to this node.

economic_capture.description: Ability of exposed suppliers to retain pricing, margin, volume, or strategic bargaining benefits.

mispricing_potential.description: Evidence-based gap between operating implications and market expectations, after valuation risk.

migration_risk.description: Risk that the constraint is released, bypassed, or migrates elsewhere; higher means greater risk.

evidence_quality.description: Recency, traceability, independence, and counterevidence resilience of the evidence set.
